headline: A Giant Cable Exposes Ireland’s AI Ambitions and Security Risks
summary: (Bloomberg) -- On the southwest coast of County Cork, where the lush green landscape gives way to the paler green of the Atlantic Ocean, Ireland is about to make its mark in the tech world again. A subsea cable is due to make landfall here, a high-capacity fiber-optic link designed to handle artificial intelligence loads for Amazon Web Services customers across Europe.Most Read from BloombergSpaceX IPO Left Mirae With No Shares on MisunderstandingSupreme Court Backs Birthright Citizenship in Blo

headline: Amazon (AMZN) Launches $1 Billion AI Engineering Unit Inside Customer Organizations
summary: Amazon Web Services is creating a new Forward Deployed Engineering division funded with US$1b. The unit will embed specialist AI engineers directly inside customer organizations. (NasdaqGS:AMZN), this move highlights how important AI services are within the wider AWS business. Instead of only offering tools and guidance from the outside, AWS plans...

Yahoo Finance Technology Editor Dan Howley breaks down the details.

headline: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI capacity, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Meta Platforms is building a cloud business to sell excess artificial intelligence computing capacity, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, as tech giants seek returns on costly AI investments amid worries about ‌overspending.  The plans are still in development and the strategy could change, the report said, citing people familiar ‌with the matter.  Meta shares rose more than 10%, easing pressure on a stock that has underperformed the S&P 500 this year with a nearly 15% ​decline as of Tuesday.

content.title: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI capacity, Bloomberg News reports

The plans are still in development and the strategy could change, the report said, citing people familiar ‌with the matter.  Meta shares rose more than 10%, easing pressure on a stock that has underperformed the S&P 500 this year with a nearly 15% ​decline as of Tuesday.
